_meta.source: Dragonlance — Chronicles, Legends, War of the Lance, Dragons of Summer Flame, 5th Age
_meta.system: AD&D 1st Edition compatible (DDG stat block format)
_meta.author_note: Stats derived from canonical Dragonlance sourcebooks with AD&D conversion by judgment. Personality descriptions in Neil Gaiman prose style.

tanis_half_elven.name: Tanis Half-Elven
abilities: Half-elven sight: infravision 60ft, surprised only on 1 in 10
Ranger tracking: 75% in wilderness
Leadership under pressure: allies +1 saves when Tanis commands
Dragonlance proficiency: +3 to hit and damage vs. dragons
Cannot be magically compelled to betray allies
tanis_half_elven.personality: He is the man who does not know what he is — half of everything, whole of nothing, and somehow the center that holds. He has a beard because elves cannot grow them and he cannot stop being half-mortal, and every time he looks in a mirror he sees the argument his parents never finished. He leads because no one else will, and hates himself for being good at it. The war found him hiding and he has been failing to hide from it ever since.

sturm_brightblade.name: Sturm Brightblade
sturm_brightblade.epithet: Knight of Solamnia — The Last True Knight
abilities: Brightblade: +2 ancestral sword, +4 vs. chaotic evil beings
Honor aura: cannot be charmed, cannot lie — ever
Knightly resolve: immune to fear at all times
STR 19: +3 to hit +6 damage
Death before dishonor: at 0 HP, fights 1 final round at full combat ability before dying
sturm_brightblade.personality: He is a knight from a time when knights meant something, which is to say he is a man perpetually out of place in his own era, armored in honor so absolute it has become a kind of loneliness. He follows a code that the world has largely stopped believing in, and follows it anyway, which is either the most heroic thing or the most heartbreaking depending on the angle. He will die for what he believes. He knows this. He has always known this. He has made his peace with it in the way that only people who haven't truly imagined it yet can.

raistlin_majere_hero.name: Raistlin Majere
raistlin_majere_hero.epithet: The Master of Past and Present — Red Robes
abilities: Hourglass eyes: sees all living things as they will appear in death — this cannot be turned off
Staff of Magius: +3, light/darkness at will, spells stored (3 slots), club 1-6 in melee
35% MR — gift and curse of the Test
Spell mastery: casts all spells at +2 levels of effectiveness
Frail body: CON 6 — any physical exertion requires CON check or 1d4 damage from coughing
See through all disguises and illusions: INT check auto-succeeds
raistlin_majere_hero.personality: He was broken on purpose, and he accepted the breaking, and that is what makes him more frightening than anything the darkness ever produced on its own. His eyes see time as a wound that never closes — everyone he looks at is already dying, already dust, and he has decided that if the world insists on being fragile he will be the most dangerous fragile thing in it. He loves his brother with a ferocity he would die before admitting and he uses cruelty like armor because kindness, once, almost killed him.

raistlin_majere_demigod.name: Raistlin Majere
raistlin_majere_demigod.epithet: Master of Past and Present — Black Robes
abilities: Time stop at will — 1/round, affects all but himself
Absorbs spells cast at him: redirects within 2 rounds
75% MR — absorbed the power of Fistandantilus
Staff of Magius (enhanced): 3-18 damage, disintegrate 1/day, plane shift 1/day
See through all illusions, disguises, and divine masks — no save
Coughing blood: at <=30% HP loses 5 HP/round (body failing under godhood's weight)
Will not harm Caramon — automatic failure on any action targeting his twin
raistlin_majere_demigod.personality: He became what he always said he would, and discovered that becoming it was not the same as surviving it. He has swallowed a dead archmage whole and wears the darkness like a second skin over a first skin that is already failing, and somewhere inside the architecture of all that power there is still a boy who was told he wasn't enough and decided to make enough mean something else entirely. He is the most dangerous man on Krynn. He is also exhausted in a way that has nothing to do with sleep.

caramon_majere.name: Caramon Majere
caramon_majere.epithet: The Big Man — Twin of Light
abilities: STR 20: +3 to hit +8 damage
Twin bond: +2 all rolls when Raistlin is within 30ft
Bodyguard instinct: auto-intercepts any attack targeting Raistlin if within 5ft
Gladiatorial training (Legends): +1 attack/round after year in the arena
Immovable: cannot be knocked prone or repositioned against his will by non-divine force
caramon_majere.personality: He is the strongest man in the room in every room he enters, and he has spent his entire life using that strength to carry someone who never asked to be carried and never said thank you and he would do it again tomorrow without being asked. He loves without condition or strategy or self-preservation, which is either the simplest thing in the world or the most complicated depending on whether you have ever tried it. He used to define himself by his brother. He is learning, slowly and with great difficulty, to be a person on his own.

goldmoon.name: Goldmoon
abilities: Blue Crystal Staff: heals 2-12 HP/touch, undead destroyed on contact, holy damage 2-12 vs. evil
First true cleric: divine healing restores lost limbs and cures magical diseases
Mishakal's blessing: 3/day call for direct divine intervention (Mishakal answers)
Cannot be permanently killed while Riverwind lives — Mishakal's covenant
Turn undead as 16th level cleric
goldmoon.personality: She walked across a world that had forgotten the gods and introduced herself to a staff that should not have worked and discovered, somewhere in the distance between faith and proof, that she was the proof. She is beautiful in the way that people who have been through something real are beautiful — not despite the cost but somehow because of it. She loves Riverwind with the certainty of someone who has seen the divine and recognized in it something she already knew from looking at him.

riverwind.name: Riverwind
riverwind.epithet: The Plainsman — Keeper of the Staff
abilities: STR 19: +3 to hit +7 damage
Plainsman tracking: 90% any terrain, never lost outdoors
Longbow mastery: 3 attacks/round with bow, +2 to hit
Distrust of magic: +2 saves vs. all magical effects
Protective rage: +4 all combat rolls when Goldmoon is threatened
riverwind.personality: He ran the length of a continent to prove himself worthy of a woman whose father said he was not, and found gods instead, and decided that was probably answer enough. He is tall and quiet and suspicious of things he cannot touch, which is most things these days, and he loves his wife with the kind of directness that people who learned love late sometimes have — nothing decorative about it, no performance, just the fact of it, solid as the plains he comes from.

laurana.name: Laurana
abilities: Dragonlance mastery: +3 to hit dragons, ignores dragon AC bonus from scales
Elven grace: DEX 20, +4 AC bonus, never surprised
Golden General: commands armies — troop morale never breaks under her leadership
Dragon riding: can ride any dragon without check after initial bond
Immune to dragon fear aura
laurana.personality: She left home a princess chasing a boy who didn't want to be caught, and came back a general who had changed the course of a war, and somewhere between those two people is a story about what it costs to grow up inside a catastrophe. She is more beautiful than anything on Krynn has a right to be and has spent years trying to get people to look past it and years more accepting that beauty, wielded correctly, is also a kind of weapon. She loves Tanis. She has never entirely forgiven him for not being simple.

kitiara_uth_matar.name: Kitiara Uth Matar
abilities: Dragon Highlord command: blue dragons obey without question
Skie bond: her blue dragon Skie grants her +2 all combat rolls when mounted
Ambition made manifest: immune to intimidation, charm, and fear
Sword mastery: 3 attacks/round, +1 attack when fighting former lovers
Never truly defeated: at 0 HP, leaves battlefield rather than dies — returns next encounter at 50% HP
kitiara_uth_matar.personality: She is the half-sibling that Raistlin and Caramon learned early not to trust and loved anyway, which tells you something about all three of them. She wants power the way some people want air — not as ambition exactly, more as a baseline requirement for existing — and she is brilliant enough to get it and human enough to throw it away for someone who doesn't deserve it. She will betray you. She will betray you knowing that you know she will betray you. She finds the honesty of this refreshing.

flint_fireforge.name: Flint Fireforge
abilities: STR 19: +3 to hit +8 damage
Master smith: identifies any metal object's origin, age, and maker by touch
Dwarven resilience: immune to poison, +4 saves vs. magic
Battle axe +3: inherited Fireforge weapon, returns when thrown
Stubborn beyond reason: cannot be persuaded to abandon his companions — any charm/compulsion auto-fails
flint_fireforge.personality: He is old and he knows it and he has decided that old means experienced rather than diminished, a distinction the enemies who underestimate him do not usually get to correct. He loves Tasslehoff Burrfoot with the specific exasperated tenderness of someone who has raised something chaotic and cannot bring himself to be sorry about it. He has smithed kings' weapons and held dying friends and crossed a world on feet that ache in the cold, and at no point has he considered any of it optional.

tasslehoff_burrfoot.name: Tasslehoff Burrfoot
tasslehoff_burrfoot.epithet: The Irrepressible Kender — Destroyer of Worlds (accidentally)
abilities: Kender fearlessness: literally cannot feel fear — immune to ALL fear effects, magical or otherwise
Hoopak: staff-sling combination, 1-6 melee or 1-6 ranged, can be anything Tas decides it is
Handling: any unattended object within 10ft may migrate into his pouches (no check — it just happens)
25% MR — kender are inherently resistant to magic that affects the mind
Pocket dimension pouches: contains improbable objects — 15% chance any mundane item is in there
Plot immunity: Tas cannot die at dramatically inappropriate moments — fate requires him elsewhere
equipment: Maps of places that don't exist yet
tasslehoff_burrfoot.personality: He is the most dangerous creature on Krynn because he has no fear and therefore no sense of scale — a dracolich and an interesting rock are equally compelling to him, which means he treats both with the same cheerful recklessness that has ended empires and infuriated gods. He is not stupid. He notices everything. He simply has a different relationship with consequences than other beings, one where they happen to other people and become stories he tells later. He loved Flint Fireforge more than he has words for, which is saying something because he has words for everything.

tika_waylan.name: Tika Waylan
tika_waylan.epithet: The Barmaid Who Became a Warrior
abilities: Skillet proficiency: improvised weapon 1-8 damage, counts as +2 vs. surprised opponents
Barmaid's eye: reads any room's social dynamics in 1 round — never surprised by betrayal
Common courage: allies within 30ft +1 morale when Tika fights alongside them
Loves Caramon: +3 all rolls when Caramon is threatened
tika_waylan.personality: She was a barmaid in the Inn of the Last Home and she became a hero the way most people become heroes — not by choosing it but by refusing the alternative when it was put in front of her. She is prettier than she thinks and braver than she knows and she loves Caramon Majere with a clarity that cuts right through every complicated thing in his life like a window letting in light. She kept score in a bar for years. She understands people. It turns out that understanding people and understanding war are not entirely different skills.

gilthanas.name: Gilthanas
abilities: Elven senses: infravision 60ft, 4 in 6 detect secret doors
Elven bow mastery: +3 to hit, double range
Silvara bond: +2 all rolls in presence of silver dragons
Elven magic: 4th level MU spells
Guilt-driven: -1 all rolls in presence of those he has wronged (he wronged many)
gilthanas.personality: He is an elf prince who spent the first half of his life being exactly as arrogant as his title suggested and the second half discovering what that cost everyone around him, and has been trying to settle the debt ever since in the way of people who are not sure the math will ever work out. He loves a silver dragon. He loves his people. These two facts are not always compatible and he knows it and chooses her anyway, quietly, without announcement, because some choices are too large for words.

derek_crownguard.name: Derek Crownguard
derek_crownguard.epithet: Knight of the Rose — Ambition in Armor
abilities: Knight of the Rose: highest Solamnic rank, commands all lesser knights
Ambition: +2 all rolls when rank or glory is at stake
Rigid honor: cannot disobey a direct order from his Knight Commander — ever
Tactical blindness: -2 all rolls when personal ambition conflicts with tactical reality
derek_crownguard.personality: He is everything the Knighthood produced when it stopped asking whether honor and ambition were the same thing. He is brave. He is capable. He is the kind of man who will lead a charge into certain death because retreat would look bad in the histories, and the histories matter more to him than the people in them. He is not a villain. He is something more ordinary and more dangerous — a man of principle whose principles have been subtly replaced with pride, and who cannot tell the difference.

fizban.name: Fizban the Fabulous
fizban.epithet: Paladine in Mortal Disguise — The Doddering Wizard
abilities: Paladine's aspect: true form is Greater God — stats above are mortal-form limits only
Forgetfulness as weapon: 20% chance any harmful action toward him simply fails to occur to the attacker
All arcane magic at will — limited only by what the mortal form can contain
95% MR
Cannot directly intervene against mortal free will — divine compact
Pyrestone hat: contains more than physics allows, produces whatever is needed
Death is an inconvenience: if this form dies, Fizban returns in 1d4 turns from a different direction
fizban.personality: He is a god wearing a fool's costume, which is either the humblest thing divinity has ever done or the most strategic, and he has been doing it long enough that he is no longer entirely certain which. He forgets things. He misplaces spells. He shows up at precisely the moment he is needed wearing the expression of someone who has no idea why he is there, and the people who believe this have never looked carefully at his eyes, which are the color of eternity on a clear day and miss absolutely nothing.

cyan_bloodbane.name: Cyan Bloodbane
cyan_bloodbane.epithet: The Dream Destroyer — Greatest of the Green Dragons
abilities: Chlorine gas breath: 50ft cone, 14d10 damage (save halves)
Dream poison: any hit requires save vs. poison or target falls into waking nightmare (-4 all rolls, 1d6 turns)
Telepathic nightmare: range 1 mile, forces target to experience their worst fear for 1 round
55% MR
Takhisis bond: serves Her Dark Majesty directly — immune to any divine power not of Takhisis
Subtle intelligence: prefers psychological destruction to physical — will talk before fighting
cyan_bloodbane.personality: He is the most intelligent dragon on Krynn and uses it the way a surgeon uses a blade — with precision, without pleasure, to cause the maximum damage to the minimum tissue. He destroyed a king with dreams rather than fire. He considers this an improvement. He has been in the service of Takhisis since before most nations existed and has developed, over millennia of obedience, something that functions like contempt for everyone including his mistress, a feeling he is far too intelligent to express.

lord_soth.name: Lord Soth
lord_soth.epithet: Knight of the Black Rose — Death Knight of Dargaard Keep
abilities: Level drain: each hit drains 1d2 levels permanently (no save)
Death wail: all within 60ft must save vs. death or be paralyzed 1d6 rounds
Impervious to non-magical weapons
Immune to fire, cold, electricity, poison, charm, sleep, fear
85% MR
Rose Knight armor: AC -8, never needs repair, regenerates 5 HP/round
Tragic compulsion: cannot leave Dargaard Keep permanently — returns within 1d10 days
Cannot be destroyed: only Soth himself choosing redemption can end him
lord_soth.personality: He could have stopped a war. He chose not to because a woman told him the truth about himself and he decided that destroying the world was preferable to sitting with what she said. This is the architecture of his damnation — not grand evil, not ambition, just a man who loved his pride more than everything else alive, and now he has neither love nor death nor the release of either, only the keep and the fire and the song the banshees sing about what he chose. He is the most tragic figure on Krynn. He would hate knowing you felt sorry for him.

huma_dragonbane.name: Huma Dragonbane
huma_dragonbane.epithet: The Greatest Knight — Who Ended the First Dragon War
abilities: Dragonlance +5: the original, forged by Reorx himself — destroys any dragon on a critical hit
Paladin of Paladine: all good divine spells available, no limit
Dragon fear immunity: absolute
Inspiration: any Solamnic Knight within 60ft fights at +3 all rolls
Willing sacrifice: if Huma voluntarily accepts death for others, all enemies in 300ft are banished
40% MR
huma_dragonbane.personality: He is the reason there are Knights of Solamnia — not the order, which existed before him, but the idea of what a knight could mean when the order was something more than men in armor competing for position. He rode a silver dragon into a battle he knew he would not survive and killed Takhisis's greatest general and ended a war, and Krynn has been telling the story ever since because it needs to believe that kind of person is possible. He was. He was also afraid. That is the part they leave out of the statues.

fistandantilus.name: Fistandantilus
fistandantilus.epithet: The Dark One — Archmage of Infinite Age
abilities: Soul drain: on successful hit, drains 1d4 years of life — he absorbs them
Bloodstone: his phylactery-equivalent, contains his essence — he cannot die while it exists
All magic at will: 30th level MU, no preparation required
80% MR
Inhabits disciples: can possess any mage who bears his bloodstone (saves at -4)
Temporal anchor: cannot be permanently erased from the timeline — paradox resistance
fistandantilus.personality: He has been alive long enough that he has stopped thinking of other people as people and started thinking of them as resources, which is the kind of thing that happens when you spend centuries treating the universe as a problem to be solved. He found Raistlin and recognized in him the only student he had ever met who might actually surpass him, and began immediately planning to consume him, because Fistandantilus's form of respect has always been hunger. He has lived so long he has forgotten whether he was ever young enough to be afraid of dying.

mishakal.name: Mishakal
mishakal.epithet: The Healer — Goddess of Healing and Light
abilities: Healing light: cures any wound, disease, or curse — unlimited
True resurrection: restores any dead being whose soul consents
80% MR
Blue Crystal Staff manifestation: can create a Blue Crystal Staff for any worthy mortal 1/year
Consort of Paladine: his divine power reinforces hers — +10 HP per turn when Paladine acts nearby
mishakal.personality: She is the reason there is healing on Krynn at all — she hid the knowledge of the true gods in a blue crystal staff and waited for a girl with enough faith to find it, which is either patience or grief depending on how you count the years. She loves the mortal world the way you love something you made and cannot stop worrying about. She heals without asking whether the healed deserve it, which is either the most divine quality or the most dangerous.

reorx.name: Reorx
reorx.epithet: The Forge — God of the Dwarves and Creation
abilities: Divine forge: creates any object in 1 round — including artifacts
75% MR
Dragonlance creation: the original Dragonlances were his work
Dwarven authority: all dwarves +2 all rolls in his presence
The Graygem: accidentally created it, cannot destroy it — this haunts him
reorx.personality: He made the world and then made the tools to improve the world and then accidentally made the Graygem which scattered chaos across existence, and has spent the rest of eternity making things to make up for it, which is perhaps the most dwarven story ever told about a god. He is loud and warm and smells of forge-fire and he loves making things with a purity that the other gods find either admirable or exhausting depending on the day.

gilean.name: Gilean
gilean.epithet: The Book — God of Neutrality and Knowledge
abilities: The Tobril: contains all knowledge — answers any question with absolute accuracy
90% MR
Balance keeper: if Good or Evil gains clear advantage, intervenes — not for either side, for balance
True neutrality: cannot be swayed by argument, love, or threat — ever
Chronicler: knows everything that has happened and is happening, nothing of what will happen
gilean.personality: He wrote everything down and decided that writing things down was more important than changing them, which makes him either the wisest being on Krynn or the most frustrating depending on whether you are currently dying and hoping for intervention. He holds the scales level between Paladine and Takhisis not because he prefers either but because he prefers balance, which is the kind of godhood that gets very little thanks and does not seem to require any.

branchala.name: Branchala
branchala.epithet: The Bard King — God of Music
abilities: Song of life: any dead being within earshot must make WIS save or choose to return
Kender patron: all kender +2 CHA in his presence, their fearlessness doubles
70% MR
Harmony: ends any conflict between good-aligned beings with 1 song (no save)
Music made manifest: his songs physically alter reality in a 300ft radius
branchala.personality: He is joy made into a god, which sounds simple until you realize that joy, genuine joy, is rarer and stranger and more resistant to despair than any weapon. He chose the kender as his people because they alone still feel wonder without effort, which tells you everything about what he values and why the other gods find him alternately delightful and maddening. His songs have ended wars. He prefers that story to the one where swords ended the same wars.

chemosh.name: Chemosh
chemosh.epithet: The Lord of Bones — God of Undeath
abilities: Death touch: save at -4 or die instantly
Lord Soth's master: commands Lord Soth — one of few beings Soth obeys
75% MR
Undead army: raises and commands all dead within 1 mile
Soul bargain: offers immortality to mortals — they become undead servants, they just don't realize it yet
chemosh.personality: He offers immortality and delivers undeath and considers this an acceptable substitution, which says everything about his theology. He collected Lord Soth the way some people collect regrets — recognizing in the death knight the most perfect monument to what happens when people choose damnation slowly, one reasonable decision at a time. He is patient. The dead are very patient. He has all the time there is.

zeboim.name: Zeboim
zeboim.epithet: The Sea Witch — Goddess of the Sea and Storms
abilities: Sea mastery: controls all weather and ocean within 500 miles
Storm wrath: 1/round, summons lightning strike 10d10 any target in sight
65% MR
Tidal fury: tsunami 1/day, 300ft wave
Daughter of Takhisis: her mother's power reinforces hers over the sea — +20 HP/round near ocean
zeboim.personality: She is Takhisis's daughter and the sea's mistress and she inherited temper from both, which means storms come when she is displeased and she is very often displeased. She is not evil the way her mother is evil — she is elemental and angry and occasionally, in her better moments, wildly beautiful, the way the ocean is beautiful the second before it kills you. She mourned her son Jasper with a grief so genuine it surprised even her.

hiddukel.name: Hiddukel
hiddukel.epithet: The Prince of Lies — God of Thieves and Corruption
abilities: Perfect deception: cannot be detected as lying by any means — divine or magical
Contract corruption: any agreement made in his presence can be twisted to his advantage
60% MR
Merchant's curse: can devalue any treasure — gold becomes worthless in his hands
Whisper network: knows every secret on Krynn — information is his currency
hiddukel.personality: He is the god of the deal that seemed fair, which is to say he is the god of every transaction you will regret. He does not lie by saying false things — he lies by arranging true things in the order most likely to destroy you, and he finds the craft of it genuinely satisfying in the way that only someone who has elevated betrayal to an art form can. He likes merchants. He respects thieves. He has nothing but contempt for the honest, who he considers amateurs.

sirrion.name: Sirrion
sirrion.epithet: The Flowing Flame — God of Fire and Change
abilities: Fire immunity: absolute
Transformation: any object he touches changes form (no save)
60% MR
Wildfire: creates uncontrolled fire storms 500yd radius 1/day
Change made divine: any being near him must save WIS each turn or feel urge to transform something
sirrion.personality: He is change that decided to become a god rather than just a force, which made him more comprehensible and considerably more dangerous. He loves fire the way some beings love a philosophy — not for what it destroys but for what it makes possible in the destroying. He is married to Shinare, goddess of industry and wealth, which is either the most compatible union in the pantheon or the most volatile, and both of them seem to find this funny.

habbakuk.name: Habbakuk
habbakuk.epithet: The Fisher King — God of Animals and Nature
abilities: Animal mastery: commands all natural animals absolutely
65% MR
Sea creature lord: all ocean creatures serve him without question
Rebirth: any natural creature slain in his presence may be reborn (50%)
Nature's balance: in natural settings, his MR increases to 85%
habbakuk.personality: He is the god of every creature that has ever looked at a human and made a reasonable decision to run, and he loves them for their sense. He is one of the gentler gods of Krynn, which does not mean passive — nature is not passive, and neither is he — but his violence is the violence of the food chain rather than the violence of ambition, which is at least honest about what it is.

takhisis.name: Takhisis
takhisis.epithet: The Dark Queen — Her Dark Majesty
takhisis.phase1: Five dragon heads: each with separate breath weapon (fire/ice/acid/lightning/poison). All five simultaneously once/round.
takhisis.phase2: True Form: 500ft wingspan, darkness radius 500yd, all good-aligned beings -4 all rolls. Summons Dragon Highlords as avatars.
takhisis.phase3: SOUL STORM: attempts to steal the souls of all defeated enemies this campaign. All PCs must save vs. death at -6 or their souls are taken. Paladine intervention: 30% chance if any PC prays.
abilities: Five dragon heads: fire/ice/acid/lightning/poison breath each 1/round
95% MR
Commands all evil dragons on Krynn absolutely
Soul capture: any slain by her become her servants permanently
Darkness absolute: 500yd radius, all light fails, good divine magic costs double
Divine compulsion: any evil-aligned being must save at -4 or obey her one command
takhisis.personality: She is the most beautiful thing you will ever see and she is doing that on purpose. She has five heads and each of them is lying to you simultaneously in a different way, and she finds the efficiency of this satisfying. She has been trying to return to Krynn since Huma drove her out with a lance and the body of the only dragon who loved her, and she wants it back — not for any complicated reason, just because it is hers and she was told she could not have it, and Takhisis has never accepted being told no.

paladine.name: Paladine
paladine.epithet: The Platinum Dragon — God of Good and Light
paladine.phase1: Platinum dragon form: immune to all evil magic, breath weapon 60ft cone of blinding light (8d10, evil beings save vs. death).
paladine.phase2: Divine decree: banishes all undead and evil outsiders within 500ft. Summons Huma's spirit to fight alongside the party.
paladine.phase3: BALANCE SACRIFICE: at 30% HP, Paladine offers to sacrifice his divinity to seal Takhisis. If accepted, he becomes mortal — Takhisis is permanently weakened. This has happened before.
abilities: Platinum breath: 60ft cone, 8d10 blinding light, evil beings save vs. death
95% MR
Commands all good dragons on Krynn
Divine compact: cannot act directly against mortal free will
True seeing: absolute — no deception possible in his presence
Fizban is his mortal aspect — both are real simultaneously
paladine.personality: He is the reason the world is worth saving, which is a burden he carries without complaint and with the specific weariness of someone who has watched it nearly end several times and had to decide, each time, whether to prevent it. He became mortal once, for love of the world, and came back, and is not entirely sure that was the right decision, and keeps making it anyway. He is opposed to Takhisis in the way that light is opposed to darkness — not with hatred, but with the simple fact of his existence.

sargonnas.name: Sargonnas
sargonnas.epithet: The Condor — God of Vengeance and Minotaurs
sargonnas.phase1: Condor form: 200ft wingspan, talons 3-30 each, shriek forces WIS save or flee in terror.
sargonnas.phase2: Vengeance manifest: every wrong ever done to anyone near him becomes a physical wound on them (1d6 per grievance).
sargonnas.phase3: MINOTAUR LEGION: 1d100 divine minotaur warriors manifest. Each has Sargonnas's rage.
abilities: 85% MR
Vengeance tracking: knows every wrong ever done to any being he meets
Minotaur lord: all minotaurs serve him absolutely
Rage empowerment: his attacks +2 for every wrong done to him (cumulative)
Consort of Takhisis: her power reinforces his — +30 HP when she is present
sargonnas.personality: He is Takhisis's husband and he resents it, which is perhaps the most honest relationship in the pantheon. He is the god of the grudge held perfectly, the wrong remembered forever, the vengeance eventually collected, and the minotaurs understand this better than any other race on Krynn because they are built the same way. He is not her servant. He is her equal in power and her inferior in ambition and he knows the difference and it infuriates him daily.

nuitari.name: Nuitari
nuitari.epithet: The Black Moon — God of Black Magic
nuitari.phase1: Black moon ascendant: all black-robe mages on Krynn gain +4 all rolls. Good magic costs double.
nuitari.phase2: Void of Nuitari: 300ft radius null magic zone except black magic.
nuitari.phase3: DARK CONVERGENCE: all black-robe mages on Krynn are drawn to his location and fight for him.
abilities: 90% MR
Black magic amplification: all evil magic +4 damage and effect in his presence
Invisible moon: Nuitari is unseen by non-mages — only black-robe mages can perceive his true form
Test of High Sorcery: oversees the Test — can alter it mid-trial
Son of Takhisis: her divine protection applies to him
nuitari.personality: He is the moon you cannot see, which is the appropriate metaphor for a god whose power is most felt by those who have already chosen darkness and need it confirmed. He does not recruit. He does not persuade. He simply makes the dark easier to use for those who have chosen it, which is a form of encouragement that asks nothing and offers everything and has never once been mistaken for kindness.

lunitari.name: Lunitari
lunitari.epithet: The Red Moon — Goddess of Neutral Magic
lunitari.phase1: Red moon ascendant: all red-robe mages +3 all rolls. Magic becomes more unpredictable.
lunitari.phase2: Lunitari's lens: reveals the true form of all illusions and disguises within 500ft.
lunitari.phase3: MAGIC WILD SURGE: all spells cast in 300ft radius produce double effect — beneficial or catastrophic equally.
abilities: 85% MR
Neutral magic mastery: balances magic between good and evil
Daughter of Gilean: his neutrality reinforces hers
Red moon power: at full, all magic +2 effectiveness regardless of alignment
lunitari.personality: She is the balance between the magic that heals and the magic that destroys, which means she is perpetually mediating between her cousins and has developed, over millennia, the patience of someone who has accepted that the argument will never end and has decided to find the ongoing argument interesting rather than exhausting. She is the most curious of the three moon gods. She finds mortals and their choices fascinating in the way that only someone with no stake in the outcome can.

solinari.name: Solinari
solinari.epithet: The White Moon — God of White Magic
solinari.phase1: White moon ascendant: all white-robe mages +3 all rolls. Undead -4 all rolls.
solinari.phase2: Solinari's light: truth compulsion in 500ft — all deceptions become visible.
solinari.phase3: CONVERGENCE OF LIGHT: white-robe mages on Krynn drawn to location. Holy light 10d10 to all evil beings in 500ft.
abilities: 85% MR
White magic mastery: good magic +4 effectiveness in his presence
Son of Paladine: his father's divine power reinforces his
Truth light: no illusion or lie survives in his full moonlight
solinari.personality: He is the moon you navigate by, which is the most quietly useful thing a god can be. He does not demand worship or sacrifice or even attention — he simply makes it easier to find your way in the dark, and has decided that this is enough, which is either humility or the longest game anyone has ever played. White-robe mages pray to him and he answers, consistently, reliably, without drama, and this consistency is, in its own way, a kind of miracle.

khellendros.name: Khellendros (Skie)
khellendros.epithet: The Storm Over Krynn — Kitiara's Dragon
abilities: Lightning breath: 90ft line, 18d10 damage (save halves)
65% MR
Overlord size: +6 AC bonus, attacks hit all in 10ft radius
Grief-driven: mourns Kitiara — any mention of her gives party 1 free round before he attacks
Storm mastery: controls weather in 100 mile radius
5th Age power: has absorbed enough dragonsteel to survive anything short of divine intervention
khellendros.personality: He was Kitiara's dragon before he was anything else, and he has spent the years since her death being several things — a Dragon Overlord, a terror, a force of destruction that swept across continents — and none of them have filled the space she left. He is the proof that dragons love, because only something that loved could grieve like this, and he has converted his grief into conquest because he was a blue dragon before he was a Dragon Overlord and conquest is the only vocabulary he ever had for feeling things.

beryllinthranox.name: Beryllinthranox (Beryl)
beryllinthranox.epithet: The Great Green — Dragon Overlord of Qualinesti
abilities: Chlorine gas breath: 100ft cone, 20d10 damage (save halves), lingering 2 rounds
70% MR
Forest corruption: plants within 1 mile warp and attack on her command
Obsessive cruelty: tortures prey before killing — all within sight must save vs. fear each round
Overlord mass: cannot be moved or grappled by any non-divine force
Qualinesti destroyer: demolished the elven capital — all elves -2 all rolls against her from grief
beryllinthranox.personality: She is what cruelty looks like at continental scale — not the cruelty of passion or revenge but the cruelty of appetite, which is worse because it isn't personal. She destroyed the elven homeland the way you clear land for something else, and the something else was never entirely clear, and that is the most frightening thing about her. She is not executing a plan. She simply cannot stop.

malystryx.name: Malystryx (Malys)
abilities: Fire breath: 150ft cone, 24d10 damage (save for half), sets terrain on fire permanently
75% MR
Volcanic creation: created the Desolation — can reshape geography 1/day
Dragon godhood attempt: at <=25% HP, attempts to ascend — becomes immune to all weapons for 1d4 rounds
Size: largest creature on Krynn — all within 30ft automatically knocked prone each round she moves
Absorbs dragon souls: each dragon she kills increases her HP by 20 permanently
malystryx.personality: She came from beyond Krynn and decided to stay because Krynn was flammable, which tells you everything about her values and her standards. She is not intelligent in the way that Cyan Bloodbane is intelligent — she does not scheme or whisper or arrange — she simply burns, with a focus and a purity that the more complicated evil cannot match. She is trying to become a god. The other Dragon Overlords find this ambitious. The gods find it annoying. Malystryx finds the distinction between those two reactions irrelevant.

draconians.name: Draconians (Bozak/Kapak/Sivak/Aurak/Baaz)
draconians.epithet: The Corrupted — Dragons Hatched from Stolen Eggs
abilities: Death throes: each type dies differently (Baaz: petrify attacker/Kapak: acid pool/Bozak: explode/Sivak: shapeshift to killer/Aurak: energy explosion 3-30)
Evil dragon nature: detect magic at will, immune to charm and sleep
Military discipline: fight in formation, +1 AC per 3 adjacent draconians
15% MR
Created from good dragon eggs: cannot be converted to good — the corruption is total
draconians.personality: They are what happens when you take something that should have been a miracle — a dragon, silver or gold or bronze, all that potential — and you corrupt the egg before it can hatch into what it was meant to be. They are the proof that Takhisis's evil is creative as well as destructive. They die spectacularly because they were made from creatures that were supposed to be extraordinary, and that is the last thing they have left from what they might have been.

shadow_wights.name: Shadow Wights
shadow_wights.epithet: Soldiers of Lord Soth — The Burning Dead
abilities: Level drain: 1 level per hit (no save)
Only +1 or better weapons damage them
Immune to fire — they burn eternally
25% MR
Lord Soth's command: act as extensions of his will, share his tactical awareness
Burning song: their death lament forces WIS save or -2 all rolls 1d4 rounds
shadow_wights.personality: They are Lord Soth's regret made into an army — knights who followed him into damnation the way soldiers follow commanders, because that is what soldiers do, and are now the proof that loyalty is not a moral position. They burn. They have always burned. They will burn until Soth finds his redemption or the world ends, whichever comes first, and they have stopped expecting either.
